description: Create a content slide with a left sidebar chapter navigation, a headline on automation-driven labor market change, a central treemap showing occupations by relative size and job loss/gain prevalence, and a bottom callout bullet list in a sidebar-left layout.

header.headline: The labor market faces structural change: Jobs that can easily be handled by automation, robotics and AI will experience declines – While others benefit
header.sub: Dynamics of occupations due to automation in the next ten years and relative sizes

main.title: Occupation dynamics treemap
main.body: Relative sector sizes and expected labor market dynamics over the next ten years. Sectors shown include Transport; Office & administrative support; Sales & related; Food & preparation service; Installation & maintenance; Construction & extraction; Other; Building maintenance; Management; Personal care; Art & entertainment; Healthcare; Architecture & engineering. Legend ranges from highest prevalence of job losses to highest prevalence of job gains.

items.text: Industry 4.0 is causing a structural change in the labor market; newly applicable technologies, like AI, bring considerable transformation potential to occupations. This development is not simply about substituting workers with robots – more broadly, it highlights new chances in the labor market

Over the next decade however, the sector that will experience the most job losses is the transport sector. Autonomous vehicles (AVs), driven by an AI system, will more readily substitute car, taxi and van drivers. It is expected that more than four million jobs are likely to be lost in a rapid transition to AVs beginning in five years time

In contrast – and driven partly by other factors – the health sector will experience an increase in labor demand. Here, robots will take on repetitive and menial tasks, saving time for healthcare professionals to focus on socio-emotional and emphatic components of healthcare

footer.source: 1) The area of the squares represent the size of the respective sector in the global economy
